title: Dimensions of Physical Quantities
subtitle: Every physical quantity expressed in terms of the 7 base dimensions

blocks.heading: What is a dimension?
blocks.body: A dimension is a qualitative description of a quantity's nature, independent of the unit chosen. Length has the dimension L whether you measure it in metres, feet, or light-years. Every physical quantity can be expressed as a product of powers of the seven base dimensions: M (mass), L (length), T (time), A (current), K (temperature), N (amount), J (luminosity).

blocks.heading: Dimensional formulas of common quantities
blocks.body:
Velocity: [LTâپ»آ¹]

Density (m/V): [MLâپ»آ³]

blocks.heading: Dimensional analysis in practice
blocks.body: Engineers use dimensional formulas to catch errors before building prototypes. If a formula predicts pressure but gives [MLآ²Tâپ»آ²] instead of [MLâپ»آ¹Tâپ»آ²], something is wrong â€” before you even run the experiment. This saved NASA billions (after learning the Mars Orbiter lesson the hard way).

blocks.heading: Derive the dimension of pressure
blocks.body: Pressure = Force أ· Area. Force = [MLTâپ»آ²]. Area = [Lآ²]. So Pressure = [MLTâپ»آ²] أ· [Lآ²] = [MLâپ»آ¹Tâپ»آ²]. Check: 1 pascal = 1 N/mآ² = 1 kg/(mآ·sآ²). âœ“

quiz.question: What are the dimensions of work (W = Fآ·d)?
quiz.explanation: Work = Force أ— displacement = [MLTâپ»آ²] أ— [L] = [MLآ²Tâپ»آ²]. This is the same dimension as energy.
